items.title: Founded in New York
items.description: Fiber Conversion Inc. opens its doors as a small textile reclamation operation, serving local garment manufacturers and mills.

items.title: Wartime Contribution
items.description: During World War II, the company plays a vital role in fiber recovery efforts, recycling materials to support the war-time economy and reduce waste during resource scarcity.

items.title: Expanded Operations
items.description: Growing demand for recycled fibers leads to expanded processing capacity and new partnerships with manufacturers throughout the Northeast.

items.title: Modernized Processing
items.description: Investment in modern fiber processing equipment allows us to handle a broader range of textile waste and produce higher-quality recycled fibers.

items.title: Sustainability Leadership
items.description: As environmental awareness grows, Fiber Conversion becomes a recognized leader in closed-loop textile recycling, partnering with companies committed to reducing their environmental impact.

items.title: Continuing the Legacy
items.description: With over 115 years of operation, we continue to innovate our processes while staying true to the principles that have guided us since day one: quality, service, and sustainability.
